title: Cube LUT Importer
description: A seamless, high-fidelity workflow for importing industry-standard .cube LUT files directly into Unreal Engine's color grading pipeline. No texture conversion required.

sections.title: Overview
body: The Cube LUT Importer is an editor utility plugin for Unreal Engine that provides a seamless and high-fidelity workflow for importing industry-standard .cube LUT files directly into the engine.
This plugin is particularly useful for workflows involving DaVinci Resolve, as it correctly parses and imports common LUT formats used in professional color grading pipelines.

sections.title: Features
body: Drag-and-Drop Importing - Simply drag your .cube file directly into the Content Browser to begin the import process.
Automatic Volume Texture Creation - The plugin automatically generates both the necessary 2D texture strip and the final, engine-ready Volume Texture asset.
High-Precision Workflow - To prevent color banding and data loss, the importer creates high-precision 16-bit floating-point textures, preserving the full fidelity of your LUT data.

sections.title: How to Use
steps: Enable the Plugin - Make sure the CubeLUTImporter plugin is installed and enabled in your project's plugin settings.
Locate Your LUT - Find the .cube file you wish to import on your computer.
Drag and Drop - Drag the .cube file from your file explorer and drop it directly into the Unreal Editor's Content Browser.
The plugin will automatically process the file and create the required texture assets in the Content Browser.

sections.caption: Enable the CubeLUTImporter Plugin in your project and restart the editor
Dragging a .cube file into the Unreal Engine Content Browser
Generated Volume Texture and 2D strip assets in Content Browser

sections.title: Technical Details
body: Floating-Point Parsing - Color values from the .cube file are read and stored as FLinearColor (32-bit floats).
16-bit Texture Creation - The data is used to create a Texture2D with the TSF_RGBA16F format, avoiding the common pitfall of quantizing LUT data into an 8-bit texture and losing precision.
Volume Texture - The 2D strip is processed into a proper 3D Volume Texture which Unreal Engine's post-process pipeline consumes natively.

sections.caption: Plugin in use within a Post Process Volume
